reasoning: The market was created shortly after the deaths of U.S. citizens Renee Good (Jan 7) and Alex Pretti (Jan 24), excluding them from the resolution criteria. The market probability was 18% on March 6. Since then, 9 days have passed without any publicly reported incidents of US citizens being fatally shot by ICE or dying in ICE custody. Given there are only 16 days remaining until the resolution deadline of March 31, 2026, and a reported pull-back in specific ICE operations (e.g. ending the Minnesota deportation surge) has been documented, the chances of the event happening within this very brief remaining window have correspondingly decayed. Scaling the earlier 18% market expectation by the remaining time supports a probability of approximately 11%.
